name: Jean-Marie Farina Extrait de Cologne
sentiment: The oldest commercially produced fragrance still in production. A light, radiant citrus cologne with herbal freshness — a living piece of perfume history.
similar: Acqua di Parma Colonia, 4711 Eau de Cologne, Atkinson's 24 Old Bond Street
unique: Bergamot and neroli lifted by rosemary — the original 18th-century cologne formula refined over 220 years.
beginner: An ideal entry-point for classic eau de cologne; wear it for its historical significance as much as its scent.
collector: The foundational reference for the Cologne Aromatic family.
source: Notes: Roger & Gallet official. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Bois d'Orange
sentiment: A clean, modern citrus-wood for everyday wear. Orange over cedar keeps it grounded without heaviness.
similar: Davidoff Cool Water Game, Lacoste Essential, Acqua di Parma Blu Mediterraneo
unique: Mandarin-orange brightness anchored by dry cedarwood — effortless and office-safe.
beginner: A safe, polished daily driver with broad appeal.
source: Notes: Roger & Gallet official. Performance: community aggregation.

name: Vétiver
sentiment: A textbook vetiver built on Grasse-house DNA — rooted and earthy without the chemical edge of some contemporaries.
similar: Guerlain Vetiver, Carven L'Homme, Azzaro Pour Homme
unique: Classic structure: citrus top, vetiver heart, dry-woods base. Unfussy and reliable.
beginner: A gentle introduction to the vetiver family — approachable before moving to Guerlain Vetiver.
source: Notes: Roger & Gallet official. Performance: community aggregation.

name: Molinard de Molinard
sentiment: A dignified 1980 fougère from the Grasse maison — lavender-led, with mossy depth that most modern aromatic-fougères have abandoned.
similar: Azzaro Pour Homme, Carven L'Homme, Guy Laroche Drakkar Noir
unique: Oakmoss in the heart gives a genuine green-earthy quality absent from post-IFRA reformulations of the same era.
collector: A snapshot of pre-IFRA classic French masculine perfumery.
source: Notes: Molinard press archives, Fragrantica community data.

name: Habanita
sentiment: One of the first tobacco-forward fragrances ever made. Smoky, powdery, animalic — a century-old formula that still provokes strong reactions.
similar: Serge Lutens Chergui, Tom Ford Beauty Tobacco Vanille, Parfums de Nicolaï Sacrebleu
unique: Pre-IFRA tobacco-rose accord from 1921 — the genetic ancestor of every modern tobacco oriental.
collector: Essential for any serious French heritage collection. The 1921 formulation is the benchmark.
source: Notes: Molinard archives, Fragrantica, Basenotes.

name: Grain de Soleil
sentiment: A sun-drenched Grasse citrus — uncomplicated, bright, and very wearable. Best picked up at the Fragonard museum shops in Grasse or Paris.
similar: Dior Eau Sauvage, Roger & Gallet Bois d'Orange, Acqua di Parma Colonia
unique: Provençal-influenced freshness from Grasse's tourist-perfumery tradition — genuine quality at accessible price.
beginner: A pleasant and safe starting point for Grasse heritage houses.
source: Notes: Fragonard official, community reviews.

name: Patchouli
sentiment: Classic Grasse patchouli — earthy, resinous, and rooted. This is patchouli as the French perfume tradition intended it, not the hippie-caricature version.
similar: Givenchy Gentleman (original), Reminiscence Patchouli, Etro Paisley
unique: Patchouli from a house that grows and processes the ingredient in-region — terroir-authentic construction.
collector: Worth experiencing for its connection to Grasse's raw materials heritage.
source: Notes: Fragonard official, Fragrantica data.

name: Gentleman de Galimard
sentiment: A reserved, Provençal fougère from the oldest continuously operating perfume house in the world (founded 1747). Lavender-geranium heart over mossy base.
similar: Azzaro Pour Homme, Fabergé Brut, Molinard de Molinard
unique: Made in Grasse since 1747 using a proprietary selection of local raw materials — the definition of heritage masculine.
collector: The oldest perfume house still operating — reason alone to add this to a serious collection.
source: Notes: Galimard official, community reviews.

name: Maharadjah
sentiment: Galimard's spiced-oriental pillar — cardamom and cinnamon over rose-sandalwood with a resinous base. Warm, romantic, unmistakably French in execution.
similar: Guerlain Shalimar pour Homme, Yves Saint Laurent Opium pour Homme, Jean Paul Gaultier Kokorico
unique: Oriental richness from a Grasse house — oud and amber structured around a classical spice pyramid.
collector: A Galimard signature that rewards seeking out on a visit to Grasse.
source: Notes: Galimard official, community reviews.

name: Cuir de Russie
sentiment: A faithful 21st-century reconstruction of a Russian-leather chypre, using birch tar and oakmoss at pre-IFRA levels. The smoky, floral, mossy combination is a dying genre done right.
similar: Chanel Cuir de Russie, Parfums Caron Tabac Blond, Knize Ten
unique: Aldehyde-lifted iris over smoky birch leather — the Oriza house formula applied to the most demanding historical accord.
collector: One of the few credible modern Russian-leather reconstructions. The birch tar is used fearlessly.
source: Notes: Oriza L. Legrand press, Fragrantica community data.

name: Chypre Mousse
sentiment: An oakmoss-rich classical chypre built to pre-IFRA standards — the bergamot-labdanum-oakmoss triad executed with scholarly precision.
similar: Guerlain Mitsouko, Chanel Pour Monsieur, Parfums Caron Pour Un Homme
unique: Galbanum-accented opening gives a sharp, green-bitter character before the mossy base takes over — authentically constructed without compromise.
collector: Essential for chypre completists — a rare modern construction that doesn't sidestep the IFRA restriction debate.
source: Notes: Oriza L. Legrand press, Fragrantica data.

name: Horizon
sentiment: Oriza's accessible masculine entry — a ginger-lavender aromatic that serves as the most wearable piece in a collection otherwise defined by challenging historicals.
similar: Hermès Terre d'Hermès, Frederic Malle Vetiver Extraordinaire, Carven L'Homme
unique: Ginger lift over a lavender-cedar spine — the structural minimalism is deceptive; the execution is precise.
beginner: The most accessible way to explore Oriza L. Legrand before graduating to the more demanding Cuir de Russie.
source: Notes: Oriza L. Legrand press, community reviews.

name: Rose d'Homme
sentiment: Parfums de Rosine's masculine thesis: that rose belongs in men's perfumery. Bulgarian rose grounded by pepper and patchouli — this reads masculine throughout.
similar: Cartier Declaration, Hiram Green Moon Bloom, Maison Margiela Replica Flower Market
unique: Pepper-patchouli base pivots the Bulgarian rose into masculine territory without hedging — the house makes its case without apology.
beginner: A strong entry point for men exploring rose-centric fragrances.
collector: House founded by Marie-Hélène Rogeon with Paul Poiret's legacy as its compass — historically important.
source: Notes: Parfums de Rosine press, Fragrantica data.

name: Zeste de Rose
sentiment: Citrus-rose built for warm months — grapefruit brightness over a soft rose heart. The lightest offering in the Rosine line.
similar: Roger & Gallet Bois d'Orange, Acqua di Parma Colonia, Bulgari Eau Parfumée au Thé Vert
unique: Citrus construction from a rose-specialist house — the brief detour from the house DNA produces something unexpectedly clean.
beginner: A safe, summery starting point for the Rosine house.
source: Notes: Parfums de Rosine press, community reviews.

name: Bandit
sentiment: Germaine Cellier's 1944 leather-chypre masterwork — sharp, animalic, austere. One of the most radical fragrances of the 20th century; still uncompromising on re-issue.
similar: Knize Ten, Chanel Cuir de Russie, Jolie Madame
unique: Galbanum-aldehyde sharpness over deep civet-leather — Cellier's fearless use of animalics places this outside the comfort zone of most contemporary perfumery.
collector: One of the canonical leather-chypre benchmarks alongside Knize Ten. Mandatory for serious collectors.
source: Notes: Robert Piguet archives, Fragrantica, Basenotes community data.

name: Bois Noir
sentiment: The contemporary masculine pillar from Piguet — dark vetiver-oud over a leather-pepper spine. Less radical than Bandit but built on the same bones.
similar: Tom Ford Beauty Oud Wood, Bleu de Chanel (Parfum), Parfums de Marly Herod
unique: Pepper-oud-vetiver triangle with Piguet's structural restraint — rich without the sweetness that dominates most modern dark-woody flankers.
collector: Worth pairing with Bandit to understand the house's 70-year arc from classical leather to modern dark-woody.
source: Notes: Robert Piguet Parfums press, Fragrantica data.

name: Snob
sentiment: A 1952 floral-chypre from a vintage French house revived in 2016. Aldehyde-lifted rose over authentic oakmoss-civet base — the revival preserves the period character faithfully.
similar: Guerlain Mitsouko, Chanel No 5 (as reference), Caron Bellodgia
unique: Civet and oakmoss used together at meaningful levels — a construction nearly impossible to source in post-IFRA reformulations elsewhere.
collector: The Le Galion relaunch (Paul Guerlain family) used original formula records — collector provenance is strong.
source: Notes: Le Galion press, Fragrantica community data.

name: Orgueil
sentiment: Carnation-spice over a warm amber-sandalwood base — Le Galion's 1946 oriental is a study in symmetry, each note given equal weight.
similar: Caron Poivre, Guerlain Shalimar, Parfums Lubin Idole
unique: Clove-carnation accord over labdanum-benzoin base — a post-war opulence that the revived house reproduces with conviction.
collector: Part of the Pauline Rolland-Guerlain revival — the 1940s orientals are the strongest pieces in the relaunched lineup.
source: Notes: Le Galion press, Fragrantica data.

name: Le Dandy
sentiment: A 1925 fougère-chypre hybrid — lavender and fern over oakmoss and vetiver. Count d'Orsay's archetype of dandified masculine elegance, preserved in a revival formulation.
similar: Caron Pour Un Homme, Guerlain Mouchoir de Monsieur, Chanel Pour Monsieur
unique: Fern-oakmoss heart is a direct antecedent of the fougère genre — wearing this is wearing the DNA of modern masculine perfumery.
collector: The Comte d'Orsay was one of history's great dandies — the fragrance lives up to the legend.
source: Notes: D'Orsay Parfums archives, Fragrantica data.

name: Tilleul
sentiment: Linden blossom-centered green fresh from the D'Orsay contemporary line — peaceful and unhurried, the olfactory equivalent of a Parisian afternoon under a lime tree.
similar: L'Artisan Parfumeur Timbuktu, Hermès Un Jardin en Méditerranée, Frederic Malle En Passant
unique: Linden (tilleul) is a rare anchor for a masculine fragrance — D'Orsay exploits a note too few houses bother with.
source: Notes: D'Orsay Parfums press, community reviews.

name: Vetyver
sentiment: Isabey's masculine cornerstone — a well-constructed vetiver with pepper lift and iris refinement. Quiet and precise.
similar: Guerlain Vetiver, Chanel Sycomore, L'Artisan Parfumeur Timbuktu
unique: Iris in the heart softens what could be a stark vetiver — the combination reads Parisian rather than austere.
source: Notes: Isabey Paris press, community reviews.

name: Fleur de Corail
sentiment: A soft, luminous floral oriental from the revived Isabey house. Rose-jasmine over warm amber — feminine-leaning but wearable for men who wear floral orientals comfortably.
similar: Guerlain Mon Guerlain, Chanel Chance Eau Tendre, Frederic Malle Portrait of a Lady
unique: Coral accord gives an aquatic mineral lift to the classic rose-amber structure — unexpected and effective.
source: Notes: Isabey Paris press, community reviews.

name: Thé Noir & Mandarine
sentiment: Chabaud's bestselling masculine — black tea and mandarin in effortless equilibrium. Brisk, clean, and more complex than its accessible price suggests.
similar: Bulgari Eau Parfumée au Thé Noir, Hermès Un Jardin sur le Nil, Heeley Sel Marin
unique: Black tea as a structural note rather than background suggestion — the result is genuinely tea-inflected, not a vague green-citrus approximation.
beginner: One of the easiest ways to explore the Grasse artisan tier without spending niche-house prices.
source: Notes: Chabaud Maison de Parfum official, Fragrantica community data.

name: Lait Concentré
sentiment: Warm condensed milk and iris — intimate, skin-close, and quietly addictive. The furthest thing from a projection monster; wears like a second skin.
similar: Serge Lutens Un Bois Vanille, Kerosene Dirty Dozen, L'Artisan Parfumeur Mure et Musc
unique: Iris-vanilla combination with an authentically milky texture — not a synthetic gourmand but a soft, credible skin fragrance.
source: Notes: Chabaud Maison de Parfum official, community reviews.

name: Café Gourmand
sentiment: Roasted coffee over tonka and patchouli — a gourmand that earns its category without becoming a dessert parody. The cardamom-pepper opening gives it genuine structure.
similar: Montale Intense Café, Profumum Roma Acqua di Sale, Commodity Coffee
unique: Grasse-made coffee fragrance: the roasted-bean accord is constructed with ingredient quality above its price point.
beginner: An ideal gateway for men who feel intimidated by gourmand fragrances — the coffee anchor makes it feel sophisticated rather than sweet.
source: Notes: Chabaud Maison de Parfum official, Fragrantica data.

name: Alahine
sentiment: Teo Cabanel's defining statement — saffron and rose over a labdanum-benzoin base of extraordinary richness. A quiet giant of French niche perfumery.
similar: Serge Lutens Feminite du Bois, Parfums de Nicolai Sacrebleu, Guerlain Samsara
unique: Saffron-rose over labdanum without the oud that this structure usually demands — Cabanel trusts the ingredients to carry the weight.
collector: One of the most technically accomplished French orientals of the 2000s. Criminally under-rated outside specialist communities.
source: Notes: Teo Cabanel press, Fragrantica, Basenotes community data.

name: Oha
sentiment: Teo Cabanel's debut — a restrained iris-lavender fougère that introduced the house's commitment to quiet elegance. Less complex than Alahine but impeccably balanced.
similar: Chanel Pour Monsieur, Guerlain Habit Rouge, Creed Original Vetiver
unique: Iris and geranium share the heart in unusual equilibrium — neither dominates, which creates a fluid, evolving structure over 4-5 hours.
beginner: A more accessible entry to Teo Cabanel before the richer Alahine.
source: Notes: Teo Cabanel press, community reviews.

name: Vétiver Bengalore
sentiment: Named for Mysore vetiver's Bengalore origins — an earthy, cardamom-accented vetiver of unusual richness. Founder Yuri Gutsatz's materials sourcing is the story here.
similar: Hermès Terre d'Hermès, Chanel Sycomore, Guerlain Vetiver
unique: Indian vetiver sourced specifically for smoky-earthy character — more rooted and mineral than Haitian vetiver constructions.
collector: Yuri Gutsatz's material-sourcing approach makes this a terroir fragrance — the ingredient provenance is verifiable and meaningful.
source: Notes: Maison Le Jardin Retrouve press, community reviews.

name: Jardin des Tuileries
sentiment: An ode to Paris's Tuileries gardens in spring — violet leaf and iris over a restrained cedarwood base. Light, literary, unmistakably French.
similar: L'Artisan Parfumeur Primeur du Thé, Maison Margiela Replica At the Barber's, Hermès Un Jardin en Méditerranée
unique: Violet leaf as the dominant green note gives this a slightly powdery, naturalistic quality distinct from conventional green-fresh constructions.
source: Notes: Maison Le Jardin Retrouve press, community reviews.

name: Lord Byron
sentiment: A 1930s Romantic-era tribute — named for Byron, smelling like his era: spiced carnation and rose over labdanum amber. The Cherigan house revival preserves its period opulence.
similar: Guerlain Shalimar, Caron Nuit de Noël, Yves Saint Laurent Opium
unique: Carnation-clove over labdanum without the musky animalics of the era — cleaner than contemporaneous 1930s formulas while retaining the period warmth.
collector: Pre-war French oriental revived with source formula integrity. The kind of fragrance that was abundant before IFRA restrictions and is now rare.
source: Notes: Cherigan Paris press, community reviews.

name: Vers le Soir
sentiment: Toward Evening — the title says everything. A heliotrope-iris powder that deepens as it wears, aldehyde-lifted over amber-vanilla. Soir in the French sense: composed and unhurried.
similar: Caron Fleurs de Rocaille, Guerlain L'Heure Bleue, Jean Patou Joy
unique: Heliotrope and iris in tandem produce an almond-powder note that reads warm rather than clinical — a subtle distinction that separates period formulas from modern reconstructions.
collector: A pre-war powdery oriental — the combination of civet, heliotrope and iris is impossible to reproduce under current ingredient restrictions.
source: Notes: Cherigan Paris press, community reviews.
